reviews.review_msg: The lectures can be disorganized, but reading the textbook and using resources like unstuckstudy ai helped me stay on top of the material. Overall, a challenging yet rewarding course.
Lectures were so useless it was basically just random derivations that he felt like he wanted to show the class.
Never ever go to lectures. They won't prepare you for the midterms or the final.
I stopped going to lectures after the first midterm and it was the best decision I could have made. I think I would fail the course if I went to lectures. Very bad explanations and useless tutorials.

This is the second class I've had with Dr. Jungic (I also had him for calculus 3) and it's been a terrible experience overall. Just really disorganized lectures and confusing explanations. I did much better this time by just reading the textbook and not going to his classes.
Not a real teacher, he just loves to show off in class with derivations that he ends up screwing up anyways. I'm glad I never have to take courses with him again.

Super passionate about math, and very caring & helpful if you have any questions!

Happy Retirement Dr. J!
Happy retirement Dr. J! The Passionate lecture will always remind me purpose of studying.

Super nice Prof, I recommend. However some of the proofs in this course might be hard to figure out on your own in a short time frame, so you might not want to cram half the course in a day or two if u want a really good grade

Veselin Jungic is the most caring, supportive professor I've ever had. He genuinely wants his students to enjoy the class. The lectures were complex and the assignments were tough but you learn the tricks to solving them in class. The group project had lots of freedom and was fun. The exam was extremely fair. I hope Dr. J. has a great retirement!
you should appreciate have Dr. J as professor.

He is an amazing prof who really cares about his students. I highly recommend going to his lectures as they were very insightful and he is very willing to answer questions during and after lectures. So long as you are not interrupting. His exams are tough but you will see similar question to the ones he does in class or you do for homework.

Dr. Jungic is by far the most passionate professors I've met to date. I don't ever rate profs or even go on this website but his passion and care for students swayed me to write this. He encourages questions and doesn't make you ever feel bad for asking them , he also makes you feel engaged and helps you understand in and out of lecture.
